Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

STT 2, 239 [Namburbu][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
o 1o 1

[x] ⸢ḪUL⸣ KI.NU₂ lu NITA lu MUNUS lu ina GE₆ lu ina ⸢kal⸣ [x x]

(o 1) [...] withdraw or reassign the evil of a sleeping place -- whether a man's or a woman's, whether during the night or late in the [...] during the siesta-time -- from their sleeping place, [...] he urinated his urine [...] his sleeping place, for the evil of that sleeping place not to reach [...], he ... [...] the bed on top of which he was lying.

o 22

[x] ⸢ina⸣ AN.BIR₉ ina KI.NU₂-šu₂-nu e-nu-u lu šu-te-[nu-u]

o 33

[x x] KI.NU₂-šu₂ KAŠ₃-šu₂ iš-ti-nu ḪUL KI.NU₂ ⸢šu?⸣-[a?-tu₂?]

o 44

[x x] ⸢la₃⸣ SA₂.SA₂ gišNU₂ ša₂ ina UGU ni-lu i-⸢ZAG?⸣-[x-x]

o 55

[x x] ana DINGIR.MAḪ ŠUBdi NINDA ina UGU U₂ giš!(MA.)KIRI₆ ta-[na-di]

(o 5) You lay down a [...] to Belet-ili. You throw bread on top of plants from the orchard.

o 66

[NIG₂].⸢NA⸣ šimLI GARan gišNU₂ tu-x-[x-x]

(o 6) You set up a censer (full) of juniper. You ... [...] the bed.

o 77

⸢ḪUL⸣ KI.NU₂ an-ni!(GAG-)i ⸢a⸣-a KUR-an-ni a-a x [x x]

(o 7) He says [...] "May the evil of this sleeping place not reach me, not ... [...]" and the evil will not reach the man.

o 88

[x x] DU₁₁.GA-ma ḪUL a-⸢na⸣ LU₂ NU KUR[ad]

o 99

[x] ⸢GI⸣.DU₈-MEŠ a-na dUTU [dASAR].⸢LU₂.ḪI GUBan⸣

(o 9) You set up [...] offering tables to Šamaš and Asalluhi.

o 1010

[x] UGU GI.DU₈ 3-⸢TA⸣.[AM₃ ...] ⸢tara⸣-[kas₂]

(o 10) You set out 3 [...] top of the offering tables.

o 1111

⸢ZU₂⸣.LUM.MA zi₃⸢EŠA⸣ [...]

(o 11) [...] dates (and) fine flour.

o 1212

[x x] šim⸢LI⸣ [...]

(o 12) [...] (full) of juniper.

o 1313

[x] ⸢BALqi₂⸣ [...]

(o 13) You libate [...]. [...]

(o 16) [...] ...

(o 18) [...] ...

[...] ⸢qu⸣-la-⸢nim⸣-ma

(o 19) [...] attend to me and let me proceed [...].

o 2020

[...] lul-lik

o 2121

[...] ⸢KAŠ₃⸣ iš-⸢ti⸣-nu

(o 21) [...] he urinated urine, [...], may it not approach, may it not reach me.

o 2222

[...] a-a ⸢DIM₄?⸣ [a]-a [KUR-an]-ni

o 2323

[... lib]-⸢bal⸣-kit [KUR]a

(o 23) [...], may it cross over the mountain.

o 2424

[...] ina SU-ia₅

(o 24) [...] from my body.

o 2525

[... li]-tal-li ina ANe

(o 25) May it keep going up into heaven [...].

o 2626

[...] ⸢ZI⸣ḫi ana ⸢KI⸣-šu₂ a-a [i]-⸢tur⸣

(o 26) [...] an uprooted [...] may it not return to its place.

o 2727

[x x li-lil₂]-⸢an⸣-ni u₂IN₆.⸢UŠ₂⸣ [lip]-⸢šur?-an-ni⸣

(o 27) May the [...] purify me, may the maštakal-plant release me.

o 2828

[... me-lam]-⸢ma⸣-ša₂-⸢ma⸣ [lum]-⸢ni lit-bal⸣

(o 28) [...] its fearsome radiance, may it take away my evil.

o 2929

[...] x [x x] x [a]-me-ru-a [x x]

(o 29) [...] ... [...] ... my observers, [...] appoint [...] for me.

o 3030

[...] ⸢šuk⸣-[na-ni]

(r 1) May [...] ... [...] not come near, may it not draw near, not [...].

* KI.MIN? x [...]

(r 11) (If) ditto ... [...], ... [...] ... [...] thus: "[...] the evil of the sleeping place."

r 1212

šim[...] TA ⸢ki⸣-a-[am ...]

r 1313

ḪUL ⸢KI⸣.[NU₂ ...]

r 1414

* KI.MIN? [...]

(r 14) (If) ditto [...], in front of the Wagon [...] ... [...] you bind ... [...] his hem.

r 1515

⸢ina? IGI? mul⸣MAR.GID₂.[DA ...] x x [...]

r 1616

x x x ⸢KA?⸣ x [... TUG₂].SIG₂?-⸢šu KEŠ₂⸣

r 1717

⸢EN₂⸣ dUTU LUGAL AN[e x x] ⸢muš⸣-te-šir AN.TA-MEŠ KI.TA-MEŠ

(r 17) Incantation. Šamaš, king of heaven [...], bringer of order to the upper world (and) the lower world, may the evil of this sleeping place [...] not come near, may it not draw near.

r 1818

⸢ḪUL⸣ KI.NU₂ ⸢an⸣-[ni-i ...] a-a TEa a-a KU.⸢NU⸣

r 1919

⸢a⸣-[a] ⸢DIM₄⸣ a-a [...] x [...] x

(r 19) May it not approach, not [...] ... [...] ....

r 2020

⸢3-šu₂⸣ DU₁₁.[GA]

(r 20) You say (this) 3 times.

r 2121

⸢TA⸣ UR₃ tur-⸢ra⸣-[ad-ma] ⸢SIG₄⸣ ana ⸢DINGIR.MAḪ⸣ [x x]

(r 21) You come down from the roof and [...] a mudbrick for Belet-ili.

r 2222

⸢U₂⸣ gišKIRI₆ ⸢ŠUB⸣[di x x TUR].⸢TUR⸣ [...]

(r 22) You throw down plants from the orchard. [...] of tiny [...].

r 2323

[x] x LA.⸢ḪA⸣.[AN-MEŠ x x] GA GU₄ ⸢SA₅-ma⸣ x [...]

(r 23) [...] ... flasks, you fill [...] with cow's milk and ... [...].

r 2424

[...] SAG [x x ki]-⸢a⸣-am ⸢DU₁₁⸣.[GA]

(r 24) [...] first-class [...]. You speak as follows.

r 2525

⸢EN₂⸣ DINGIR.⸢MAḪ AMA ba⸣-[ni-tu] ⸢at⸣-ti-[ma]

(r 25) Incantation. Belet-ili, mother, you are the creator.

r 2626

[x] KI.⸢NU₂⸣ [an]-ni-[i ...]

(r 26) [...] of this sleeping place [...] draw near, may it not approach, [...].

r 2727

r 2828

(r 28) [...] the river, may it cross over the mountain, may it withdraw [...].

r 2929

[x qut]-⸢ri li-tel⸣-li ina [x x]

(r 29) [...] smoke, may it keep going up into [...].

r 3232

[... me]-lam-ma-ša₂-⸢ma⸣ lit-⸢bal⸣ [x x]

r 3333

[x KI].⸢NU₂⸣ ša₂ a-mu-ra ina MUNUS.[SIG₅ ...]

(r 33) [...] into good fortune the [...] of the sleeping place that I saw.

r 3434
